title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces a middle-seat-turned-table design on its new Airbus A321XLR aircraft.
The middle seat in Economy Plus transforms into a shared table, providing more space for window and aisle passengers.
United plans to offer these redesigned seats on 50 A321XLR jets with domestic flights starting this fall.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market for $2.76 million
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The 'Gilmore Girls' pilot house at 31 Victoria Ave in Markham, Ontario, is on sale for $2.76 million USD.
Originally constructed around 1875, the home features four bedrooms, four bathrooms, and a Gothic Revival style.
A 550-square-foot barn, zoned for commercial use, is included on the property, adding historic charm to the listing.

title: Longtime Maine soccer coach recognized by FIFA as ‘Unsung Hero’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Gary Newell, a Maine soccer coach, was honored by FIFA as an 'Unsung Hero' at the 2026 World Cup.
Newell was recognized for his decades-long contribution to youth soccer development in Boston.
England midfielder Jude Bellingham sent a special video message commending Newell’s work with FIFA's anti-doping program.

title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old willow tree collapsed in Boston Public Garden after exceeding its typical lifespan.
No injuries occurred during the collapse; the tree was previously deemed structurally sound by routine inspections.
Friends of the Public Garden manage trees with regular care; replanting efforts occur every spring.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New speed boards in Jamaica Plain and Charles River Esplanade track cyclist and vehicle speeds to enhance safety.
Speed boards collect data on path user volume to help officials manage and assess traffic on state properties.
Installations are in response to resident concerns over cyclist speeds; similar setups exist across Massachusetts trails.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 4,000 people signed a letter urging Boston Mayor Michelle Wu to enhance street safety after cyclist Louisa Gag was killed.
Mayor Wu announced plans for increased safety measures at a vigil, including stricter enforcement against vehicles blocking bike lanes.
Advocates criticize the city for stalling crucial safety projects, demanding progress on stalled projects and restored transportation funding.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police are investigating after Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, were found dead in West Bath.
The Sagadahoc County Sheriff’s Department conducted a welfare check on July 13, discovering the deceased couple.
Autopsies are being conducted to determine the cause of death, with police stating there is no public danger.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2024 Honda CR-V owner experienced issues with interior lights, headlights, and hatch locking post body shop repairs.
Issues may stem from lost programming in the car's ADAS systems, requiring recalibration by a technician.
The owner is advised to contact their insurance for a potential supplemental claim on the repairs.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Officers in Dorchester came under fire during a traffic stop; no injuries were reported, and the suspect was apprehended.
Police identified Rasiel Joel Carbuccia as the suspect, charging him with multiple firearm offenses and prior outstanding warrants.
Boston Police Patrolmen's Association urged officials to condemn the attack, asserting officers' rights to safety while on duty.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a crash on I-495 North in Andover Wednesday night.
The driver and three children from a minivan were hospitalized, with the driver suffering serious injuries.
The crash involved another car, a sedan, which had significant damage, but its driver was not injured.
